author: @ChadHowarth
text: While I appreciate your efforts, using a proper bifilar coil in a Joule thief is counter productive. In the way that you have used it, current is flowing in the same direction through both parts of the coil. You may as well use a standard configuration with a ferrite ring. Bifilar coils are best at suppressing back EMF when current is passed in opposite directions through each half of the coil. They cancel out each others' magnetic field in this configuration.

replies.author: @JenkoRun
replies.text:
If you read Tesla's patent he specifically designed it to run the current in the same direction in both halves of the coil. The method of wiring it in the opposite direction was never mentioned or proposed by him, and obviously he tested the device before making a patent.

I don't know why people want to change his coil to do that, but it's definitely not what Tesla designed the coil to be for cancelling self-induction.

author: @iluzyon1
text: Cool one Robert :D ...You probably already know it but if you take the LED out of the circuit, your pancake coil will ''radiate'' energy that you can pick up with other coils wirelessly. You can see it all over the place on youtube. A wirelees joule thief :)
